title: Design System: AI SDK Flutter UI

tertiary.canonical: Material 3 ColorScheme.tertiary (representative: #7D5260), used only on the tool-approval shield

body.purpose: Message content; flush assistant prose at line-height 1.5. Inherited from host TextTheme.

title.purpose: Tool names and card titles, on tabular figures.

label.purpose: Section labels, usage values, result/error tags.

mono.purpose: Pretty-printed tool input/output only. The one explicit font family.

shadows.name: flat-by-doctrine
shadows.purpose: All bubbles, cards (elevation: 0), banners, chips, and the composer are shadowless. Depth is tonal layering + outlineVariant hairlines.

shadows.name: fab-elevation
shadows.purpose: The single intentional elevation, on the scroll-to-bottom FAB only, because it floats over scrolling content.

motion.name: curve-emphasized
motion.purpose: Slightly snappier deceleration for larger reveals.

motion.name: curve-gentle
motion.purpose: Scale-ins and pops. No overshoot, never bounce.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Tap-down feedback on every interactive control; springs back on release.

motion.name: entrance
motion.purpose: New messages, banners, and object values ease in once; identity-tracked so scrolling never replays it.

motion.name: streaming-cursor
motion.purpose: Inline caret on the in-flight assistant message — a soft breath, not a hard blink.

motion.name: typing-wave
motion.purpose: Three-dot indicator before the first token; cross-fades into the streaming text.

motion.name: send-stop-morph
motion.purpose: Send morphs into stop as one object changing state.

motion.name: auto-scroll
motion.purpose: Transcript auto-scroll and scroll-to-bottom FAB; FAB also scales+fades in on appear.

motion.name: suggestion-stagger
motion.value: 420ms controller, Interval per chip capped at 6 steps
motion.purpose: Empty-state prompt chips ease in on a short stagger.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: First-class path on every animation. Mandatory, not a branch.

haptics.name: light
haptics.purpose: Send, stop, copy-success, scroll-to-bottom, error retry.

haptics.name: selection
haptics.purpose: Approve/deny, reasoning toggle, chip and attachment taps.

components.name: User Bubble
components.description: The user's message; the one place the host primary accent fills a surface.
components.html: How do I cancel my plan?
components.css: .ds-bubble-user { display: inline-block; max-width: 78%; background: #6750A4; color: #FFFFFF; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; padding: 10px 14px; border-radius: 16px 16px 4px 16px; }

components.name: Assistant Turn
components.description: Bubbleless assistant reply: a small marker leads flush prose, so long answers read as content, not chat chrome.
components.html: You can cancel anytime under Settings → Billing. Your plan stays active until the end of the cycle.
components.css: .ds-assistant-turn { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; padding: 6px 0; } .ds-marker { width: 26px; height: 26px; border-radius: 50%; background: #E6E0E9; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex: none; } .ds-assistant-text { margin: 0; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; color: #1D1B20; }

components.name: Composer Field
components.description: Filled, borderless pill input. The filled primary send button morphs into a stop square (fade + scale) while streaming; both press-scale with a light haptic.
components.html: Message…
components.css: .ds-composer { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-field { flex: 1; background: #E6E0E9; color: #49454F; font-size: 14px; padding: 12px 18px; border-radius: 24px; } .ds-send { width: 40px; height: 40px; border: none; border-radius: 50%; background: #6750A4; color: #FFFFFF; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.12s ease-out, filter 0.12s ease-out; } .ds-send:hover { filter: brightness(1.05); } .ds-send:active { transform: scale(0.96); }

components.name: Tool Call Card
components.description: A tool call with pretty-printed input and a result row; flat (elevation 0), hairline-bordered, with a tonal code tile inside (no nested card). The result status icon scale-springs in.
components.html:
getWeather
{ "city": "Kyoto" }
Result 18°, clear
components.css: .ds-tool-card { border: 1px solid #CAC4D0; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px; color: #1D1B20; } .ds-tool-head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-tool-name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-code { background: #E6E0E9; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px; font-family: monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.4; margin: 8px 0 0; white-space: pre-wrap; } .ds-tool-result { font-size: 12px; color: #49454F; margin-top: 12px; } .ds-tool-result span { font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Source Chips
components.description: A wrap of citation chips; the link icon carries the lone primary accent. Each press-scales and fires a selection haptic.
components.css: .ds-chips { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 6px; } .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #CAC4D0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 5px 10px; font-size: 12px; color: #49454F; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.12s ease-out, transform 0.12s ease-out; } .ds-chip:hover { background: #ECE6F0; } .ds-chip:active { transform: scale(0.96); }

components.name: Error Banner
components.description: Inline error state with retry/dismiss; errorContainer surface paired with onErrorContainer ink. Eases in on appear.
components.html: Something went wrong.
components.css: .ds-error { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; background: #F9DEDC; color: #410E0B; border-radius: 12px; padding: 10px 12px; } .ds-error-msg { flex: 1; font-size: 14px; } .ds-error-retry { background: transparent; border: none; color: #410E0B; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; }

components.name: Usage Pills
components.description: Token usage as small tonal pills with tabular figures; null counts omitted.
components.css: .ds-usage { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 6px; } .ds-pill { background: #E6E0E9; border-radius: 8px; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 11px; color: #49454F; } .ds-pill b { font-weight: 500; color: #1D1B20; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; margin-left: 4px; }

narrative.overview: A Flutter UI library that conducts a genuinely rich performance — streaming text, reasoning, tool calls, human-in-the-loop approvals, source citations, token usage — and then steps out of the spotlight. It owns no palette and no typeface; every color is a Material 3 ColorScheme role and every text style a TextTheme role, resolved from the host app's Theme.of(context). The transcript reads as a natural conversation: the user's message in a soft bubble, the assistant flush (no bubble) as readable prose behind a small marker. The craft lives in structure, rhythm, restraint, and timing. Motion is a material here, not a finish — Apple-grade continuity over popping, gentle ease-out, press + haptics, and a first-class reduced-motion path, all built from core Flutter. It rejects the heavy branded chat SDK look, the dependency-bloated aesthetic, and the generic 'AI made this' chat template.
keyCharacteristics: Theme-deferential: zero owned colors or fonts; pure Material 3 role consumption.
Natural transcript: user bubbles, flush assistant prose with a minimal marker.
Flat and tonal: surface-container layering + hairline borders, no shadows (one functional FAB excepted).
Motion as material: continuity, gentle ease-out, press + haptics, mandatory reduced-motion path — all core Flutter.
Dependency-light: heavyweight capabilities are callbacks/slots, never bundled.

rules.name: The Borrowed Color Rule
rules.body: The library defines no color. Every surface, text, and accent is a ColorScheme role from Theme.of(context). A hardcoded color literal is a bug, not a style choice.

rules.name: The On-Pair Rule
rules.body: A role is never used without its on- counterpart (primary/onPrimary, errorContainer/onErrorContainer, surfaceContainerHigh/onSurface). This guarantees contrast survives any host palette.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Only primary carries accent weight, on <=10% of any screen. Its rarity makes the send button and user bubble read as the live edge of the conversation.

rules.name: The Inherited Voice Rule
rules.body: Type comes from the host. The library assigns roles and reading line-height; it never sets a font family (except monospace for serialized data) and never hardcodes a font size outside the Material scale.

rules.name: The Tabular Numerics Rule
rules.body: Tool names and token counts always use tabular figures. Conversational prose never does.

rules.name: The Tonal-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth is layered tonally and fenced with hairlines, never with shadow. The only elevated element in the entire library is the scroll-to-bottom FAB.

rules.name: The Motion-As-Material Rule
rules.body: State changes flow, never cut: cross-fade or morph instead of hard swap, gentle ease-out (never bounce), every control answers a press with a light haptic, and every animation has a reduced-motion fallback. Built from core Flutter, no animation dependency.

dos: Do resolve every color from Theme.of(context).colorScheme and every text style from textTheme — nothing hardcoded.
Do pair each role with its on- counterpart so contrast holds on any host theme.
Do render the assistant flush (no bubble); reserve bubbles for the user (and standalone tool messages).
Do convey depth tonally and fence it with outlineVariant hairlines; keep cards at elevation 0.
Do cap card corners at 12px; reserve full-pill rounding for the composer input and chips/tags only.
Do set tabular figures on tool names and token counts.
Do treat motion as a material: continuity, ease-out only, press + light haptic on controls.
Do give every animation a reduced-motion fallback and build it from core Flutter.
Do expose attachments, link-opening, and markdown as callbacks/slots — never as bundled dependencies.
donts: Don't hardcode a color or font — the library must never stamp its own identity over the host.
Don't put the assistant in a bubble in the default transcript — flush prose is the voice.
Don't build toward a heavy branded chat SDK look (Intercom/Drift-style, powered-by bubbles) that resists theming.
Don't add dependencies for image pickers, url launchers, markdown engines, or animation — that is the dependency-bloated anti-reference.
Don't ship the generic 'AI made this' chat template: gradient message bubbles, glassmorphism, or novelty animation.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing, or a hard on/off blink — motion is gentle ease-out and the cursor breathes.
Don't use drop shadows for depth — the only elevated element is the scroll-to-bottom FAB.
Don't nest a card inside a card, or use a border-left/side-stripe accent — full hairline borders or tonal fills only.
Don't over-round: a 24px+ radius on a card or tool tile is forbidden (the composer pill input is the sole intentional exception).
